3
GEORGE W. ROMNEY BUILDING • 111 SOUTH CAPITOL AVENUE • LANSING, MICHIGAN 48909 
www.michigan.gov 
Dear Participant, 
We are thrilled to welcome you to ACE Michigan, an event organized by the Organization of 
American States (OAS), the Department of Commerce and the Department of State of the United 
States of America, and the Michigan Economic Development Corporation on behalf of the State of 
Michigan. 
Team Michigan is thankful for the opportunity to serve as your hosts and extend our patented 
Midwest hospitality to you over the next several days, connecting with Michigan’s business-friendly 
environment and exceptional quality of life. 
We believe our ACE Michigan programming will result in many new connections and partnerships, 
providing the chance to strengthen existing relationships while fostering new ones. 
Thank you for your participation in ACE Michigan – we are conﬁdent you will experience and enjoy 
the many opportunities that Pure Michigan has to offer. 
Gretchen Whitmer 
Governor, State of Michigan

4
Welcome from 
the ACE Committee
Dear distinguished leaders from the Americas and beyond, 
We are honored to welcome you to the Americas Competitiveness Exchange 
(ACE) Michigan 2024. ACE is a powerful mechanism that fosters cooperation 
and creation of new partnerships to strengthen economic development in 
the Americas. 
The ACE Committee is comprised of representatives from the General 
Secretariat of the Organization of American States (OAS) and the United 
States Government Department of Commerce (Economic Development 
Administration and International Trade Administration) and the Department 
of State (Permanent Mission to the OAS). 
Throughout your week in Michigan May 5-10, 2024, you will have the 
opportunity to explore and expand on potential collaborations with 
Michigan’s vibrant industries, including Agribusiness, Life Sciences, Advanced 
Manufacturing, Automotive and Mobility, and Aerospace. 
We want to emphasize the importance of nurturing the connections that 
you establish over the course of this week to ensure you make the most of 
your time in Michigan. We are excited for what awaits us in Michigan and 
the connections that will emerge from this week. To facilitate meaningful 
and strategic engagement throughout the program, we have compiled this 
Tripbook to share key information about the ACE Michigan agenda and 
participants. We encourage you to use this resource as a compass to identify 
potential business partners for trade deals, research and development, new 
ventures, soft-landing arrangements, technical exchanges, and any other 
opportunities for collaboration. 
The ACE Committee wishes to express its most sincere gratitude to the State 
of Michigan, for the exceptional agenda they have prepared for this important 
exchange in collaboration with other key stakeholders.
We look forward to our work together. 
Sincerely, 
The ACE Committee
Americas
Competitiveness
Exchange

5
MICHIGAN
OVERVIEW
 Diverse and skilled workforce: With the highest concentration of engineers in 
the nation and a top-ten skilled trades workforce, Michigan is home to a wealth 
of highly skilled talent. Through proactive workforce development initiatives, 
Michigan is able to collaborate with businesses to train talent for future jobs, 
driving innovation and growth.
 Business-friendly environment: Michigan ranks among the top three states for 
foreign direct investment (FDI), and Detroit leads as the number one metropolitan 
area for FDI projects, according to Site Selection magazine. Michigan is home to 
more than 1,400 foreign companies with more than 5,000 locations throughout 
the state.
 Access to global and regional markets: Michigan is within 500 miles of nearly 
half the U.S. and Canadian commerce centers providing unparalleled access to 
market. Michigan serves as a premier gateway for businesses worldwide.
 Connect with global supply chains: Michigan has the greatest concentration 
of original equipment manufacturers (OEMs) in the U.S. The automotive and 
semiconductor industries continue to flourish, attracting corporate expansions 
and relocations, bolstering supplier networks, and increasing the density of 
industry ecosystems.
 Quality and affordable lifestyle: Michigan ranks as the fourth most affordable 
state in the U.S. Its cost of living is 10 percent lower than the national average, 
offering opportunities to live in vibrant communities while enjoying a high quality 
of life and global connectivity.
 Excellence in research and education: More than 2,000 PhDs are awarded 
annually by universities in Michigan, nearly twice the national average. 
Additionally, The University Research Corridor (URC), comprising Michigan State 
University, the University of Michigan, and Wayne State University, is one of the 
nation’s top academic research clusters and the leading engine for innovation in 
Michigan.
What Sets Michigan Apart:

6
Advanced 
Manufacturing
Michigan boasts cutting-edge manufacturing 
capabilities, from precision engineering to 
smart factories.
Aerospace
Michigan’s aerospace industry spans 
defense, commercial aviation, and space 
exploration. Join us to discuss aerospace 
manufacturing, satellite technology, and 
the future of space tourism.
Agribusiness
Michigan fertile lands and agricultural 
expertise create a vibrant agribusiness 
ecosystem, from farm-to-table 
innovations to sustainable practices.
Life Sciences
With world-class research institutions, a robust 
healthcare sector, and a focus on biotechnology, 
Michigan is at the forefront of life sciences. Join us to 
explore collaborative research, clinical trials, and more.
Automotive 
and Mobility
As the birthplace of the automotive industry, Michigan 
continues to lead in mobility solutions. Let’s envision 
the future of connected and autonomous vehicles, 
sustainable transportation, and urban mobility.
ACE Michigan Featured 
Industry Clusters

7
ACE SITE VISITS
Detroit Diesel
Detroit Institute of Arts (DIA)
Connect with the industry-leading original 
equipment manufacturer driving innovation in the 
industry. Discover how our state-of-the-art 
engines, axles and transmissions are designed 
for optimized performance and efficiency.
Get ready to connect at the DIA, home to a world-
class collection of over 65,000 works of art. Admire 
notable acquisitions like Diego Rivera’s Detroit 
Industry fresco cycle, which Rivera considered 
his most successful work, and Vincent van 
Gogh’s Self-Portrait. Build connections 
and celebrate the power of art to 
bring people together.

8
Michigan State University (MSU)
Meijer Garden (Grand Rapids)
Peckham Industries
Join the Spartan community at MSU, founded in 
1855 as the Agricultural College of Michigan, 
was the first land grant university in the 
U.S. MSU welcomes over 50,000 students 
each year. Explore R&D projects at the 
Facility for Rare Isotope Beams, and 
discover innovative programs in 
agribusiness, life sciences, 
logistics and more.
Connect with leaders, nature, and art at the world-
renowned sculpture and horticultural landscapes 
of Frederik Meijer Gardens & Sculpture 
Park. Join us to inspire new ideas, build 
connections, and cultivate collaboration 
in a breathtaking setting of stunning 
indoor and outdoor gardens and 
sculpture galleries.
Engage with a recognized leader in vocational 
rehabilitation and inclusive culture. Ranked #11 
on Fortune Magazine’s Great Place to Work list, 
Peckham is an award-winning organization, 
recognized for its workforce development 
and quality employment practices. Share 
and explore innovative solutions for 
regional workforce growth and 
collaboration opportunities.
MSU Grand Rapids Innovation Park
Collaborate with innovators and experts to shape 
the future of healthcare at the MSU Grand 
Rapids Innovation Park. A collaborative health 
innovation hub for biomedical research, 
bioengineering and health technology. 
This enterprising ecosystem is rich with 
opportunities for transformational 
health discoveries that will help 
people live better and longer.

Amway
Grand Valley State University (GVSU) 
Engage with the world’s largest direct selling 
company with presence in 100 countries 
through Independent Business Owners. 
Amway offers a vast platform for 
collaboration and growth in nutrition, 
beauty, personal care, and home 
products.
Connect with like-minded individuals and 
explore the exciting possibilities in life 
sciences and Medbio. GVSU is known 
for its innovative teaching, cutting-
edge research, and dedication 
to fostering an inclusive 
and supportive campus 
community.
Steelcase
Amway Grand 
Join the dynamic community at SteelcaseGrand 
Rapids location. Home to more than 2,000 
employees from various backgrounds and 
expertise, buzzing with creativity and 
collaboration. Engage with designers, 
researchers, marketers, innovators, 
engineers, and professionals in 
many fields to share ideas and 
seek new collaboration. 
Experience the rich history of the Amway Grand 
Plaza Hotel, a Grand Rapids landmark. With its 
stunning architecture, beautifully restored, this 
iconic hotel is the perfect setting to connect 
with others and explore new partnership 
opportunities.

10
Kellogg’s
As a leader in the food industry, Kellogg’s is at the 
forefront of addressing global food security and 
sustainability challenges. Their food products 
are manufactured and marketed in over 
180 countries, and it is one of the most 
recognizable brands in the world. Explore 
new possibilities and discover how 
collaboration can shape the future 
of food production.
Duncan Aviation
American Center for Mobility
University of Michigan (Michigan Stadium)
Connect with the largest privately owned 
business jet service provider in the world. 
With complete maintenance, repair and 
overhaul service offerings at three U.S. 
facilities as well as through regional 
shops and mobile teams worldwide. 
Duncan Aviation helps business 
aircraft operators get the most 
value from their aircraft 
ownership. 
Join the future of Mobility. Located on a historic 
500-acre site in Ypsilanti, ACM offers a 
collaborative hub for innovation and 
technology. Connect with industry leaders, 
explore cutting-edge test environments, 
and discover new possibilities in an 
advanced mobility proving ground.
The Big House” (UMich stadium) is the largest stadium 
in the United States with a seating capacity of more 
than 107,000. Founded in 1817, UMich is one of the 
top public universities in the US. Known for its 
academic excellence, innovative research, 
and iconic football team, UMich is a world-
renowned institution that shapes leaders, 
innovators, and citizens of the world.

11
Newlab/Michigan Central
TechTown Detroit
FANUC
Motown Museum
Explore the 30-acre campus of Michigan Central, a 
hub for collaboration innovation district located 
between the historic neighborhoods of 
Corktown and Southwest Detroit. Connect 
with a growing community of visionaries, 
innovators, and change-makers building 
urban transportation solutions that 
advance a more sustainable and 
equitable future.
Wayne State University’s innovation hub 
empowers early-stage to growth-stage 
businesses and tech entrepreneurs through 
programs, education, and resources. 
Connect with like-minded innovators 
and engage with top entrepreneurs 
from Michigan and the Americas.
A pioneer in the world of robotics and automation at 
FANUC, the global leader in CNC systems, robotics, 
and ROBOMACHINEs. Pursue collaborations 
with FANUC to upskill the workforce and 
prepare students for high-tech careers in 
advanced manufacturing. 
This legendary museum is the birthplace of Hitsville 
U.S.A., founded by Barry Gordy. As the ongoing 
heartbeat of the Motown legacy, it’s the perfect 
setting to come together and solidify the 
connections and new partnerships made 
throughout the program.

updated as new information is received. If you would like to 
make any changes to your information, kindly send a request 
to cparga@oas.org, cc: msolis@oas.org, lbenitez@oas.org, 
egomez@oas.org, jfonseca@oas.org. 
We will take your changes into consideration as the online 
version is updated.
